text.title: Claude Code Hooks Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of claude-code-hooks enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Hooks live in settings.json (user or project scope) — never in CLAUDE.md or memory.
rule.rationale: CLAUDE.md is read by the model; hooks are read by the harness. Wrong file = no execution.

rule.statement: Security-sensitive hooks (secrets, deny-list, exfil block) use PreToolUse with non-zero exit to deny.
rule.rationale: PostToolUse runs after the action — too late to block.

rule.statement: Hook commands are non-interactive and time-bounded (≤10s typical).
rule.rationale: Interactive or long-running hooks freeze the agent.

rule.statement: Hook scripts log to a known path; failures are recoverable and logged, not silent.
rule.rationale: Silent hook failures look like 'Claude is broken' to the user.

rule.statement: Hook config is versioned in the repo for project-scope hooks; user-scope hooks documented in dotfiles.
rule.rationale: Unversioned hooks drift across team members and machines.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
